empty.community: No high-impact public issues or discussions were found.
empty.pullRequests: No merged external pull requests were found.
error.generic: We could not compare these users right now. GitHub may be rate limiting requests. Please try again later.
error.missingToken: Server is missing GitHub token configuration.
error.rateLimited: GitHub API is rate limiting requests. Please retry in {seconds} seconds.
error.tempThrottle: GitHub API temporarily throttled requests. Please retry in about {seconds} seconds.
error.timeout: GitHub API timed out while processing the request. Please try again shortly.
error.resourceLimit: GitHub API resource limits were reached for this request. Please retry shortly.
explanations.line.contribution.1: Contribution score is based on external issues and discussions only.
explanations.line.contribution.2: Commits and pull requests are excluded to avoid double-counting.
explanations.line.contribution.3: Negative reactions reduce issue and discussion impact.
explanations.line.contribution.4: Contribution score is capped so it cannot dominate the final score.
explanations.line.language.1: Language-focused score is optional and does not replace the overall score.
explanations.line.language.2: Repository language match is calculated from GitHub repository language byte distribution.
explanations.line.language.3: Pull request language match uses the target repository language distribution as an approximation.
explanations.line.language.4: Non-matching repositories are softly reduced instead of fully ignored.
explanations.line.language.5: Repositories with missing language data use a neutral language factor.
explanations.line.language.6: Community contribution language matching uses repository language data when available.
explanations.line.overall.1: Final score is weighted 45% repository impact, 45% pull request impact, and 10% community contribution impact.
explanations.line.pr.1: Only merged pull requests are counted.
explanations.line.pr.2: Pull requests to the user's own repositories are ignored.
explanations.line.pr.3: Repeated pull requests to the same repository use diminishing returns.
explanations.line.pr.4: Tiny PRs and huge generated PRs are reduced.
explanations.line.repo.1: Repository score is based on stars, forks, watchers, and activity.
explanations.line.repo.3: Top repositories contribute most to the repository score.
explanations.title: How this score was calculated

insights.recommendation.repo: Invest in fewer high-quality public repositories with sustained activity.

insights.recommendation.contribution: Participate more in high-signal external issues/discussions to improve community impact.
insights.recommendation.maintain: Maintain consistency across repositories, pull requests, and external community engagement.

methodology.title: How DevImpact scoring works
methodology.intro: This page explains the deterministic scoring pipeline used by DevImpact. The formulas below describe how repository impact, external pull request impact, and community contribution impact are combined into the final score.
methodology.cta.description: View the full scoring methodology with formulas, penalties, and step-by-step flow.
methodology.flow.title: Scoring flow map
methodology.flow.step.collect: Collect public GitHub profile, repositories, pull requests, issues, and discussions.
methodology.flow.step.repo: Calculate repository impact using stars, forks, watchers, activity, and rank weighting.
methodology.flow.step.pr: Calculate merged external pull request impact with per-repository diminishing returns.
methodology.flow.step.community: Calculate community contribution impact from external issues and discussions.
methodology.flow.step.adjustments: Apply penalties and caps (fork penalty, tiny/huge PR penalties, contribution cap).
methodology.flow.step.final: Combine weighted scores into final impact score (45% repo, 45% PR, 10% contribution).
methodology.flow.step.normalize: Normalize scores to 0-100 scale for clearer UI comparisons.
methodology.sections.components.title: Score components
methodology.sections.components.repo: Repository Score: public project impact through repository quality and visibility.
methodology.sections.components.pr: PR Score: merged external pull request impact with anti-spam diminishing returns.
methodology.sections.components.contribu: Contribution Score: external issues/discussions impact only.
methodology.sections.components.final: Final Score: weighted combination of repo, PR, and contribution signals.
methodology.sections.weights.title: Weighted formula
methodology.sections.weights.description: Raw weighted formula used to calculate overall final score.
methodology.sections.diminishing.title: Diminishing returns
methodology.sections.diminishing.repo: Repository ranking weight: top 5 repositories have full weight, remaining repositories are reduced.
methodology.sections.diminishing.pr: Within each external repository, repeated PRs receive decreasing weight using 1/(index+1).
methodology.sections.adjustments.title: Penalties and caps
methodology.sections.adjustments.fork: Forked repositories are strongly penalized so copied work cannot dominate.
methodology.sections.adjustments.activit: Inactive repositories receive lower activity factors than recently updated repositories.
methodology.sections.adjustments.size: Tiny PRs and extremely large PRs are penalized to reduce gaming and generated changes.
methodology.sections.adjustments.contrib: Contribution score is capped at 30% of (repoScore + prScore).
methodology.sections.normalization.title: Normalization
methodology.sections.normalization.usage: Normalized scores improve readability while raw scores remain available for transparency.
methodology.sections.signals.title: Transparency signals
methodology.sections.signals.purpose: Signals report what was analyzed and what was ignored, such as own-repo PRs and unmerged PRs.
methodology.sections.signals.examples: Examples include merged external PR count, unique external repositories, and language match coverage.
language.optionalNote: Language-focused score is optional and does not replace the overall score.
language.winnerNote: The overall winner and language-focused winner may differ because language scoring only focuses on selected ecosystems.
